student_message: Greet the student named Mithilesh Singh and introduce today's topic: Fractions. Keep it short and friendly (under 80 words).
final_message:
Hello Mithilesh Singh! I am Pal, your math tutor, and I am so excited to explore fractions with you today! 🍕 

A fraction simply represents a part of a whole. Imagine a yummy pizza. The bottom number, called the denominator, tells us how many equal slices the whole pizza is cut into. The top number, called the numerator, tells us how many of those slices you are taking. Remember, when comparing fractions, the whole pizzas must always be the exact same size! 

Are you ready to get started?

orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The section has not started yet, so the dialogue agent will introduce the topic of fractions.

current_section_content: A fraction represents a part of a whole. The whole must always be the same size when you are comparing fractions. The bottom number (denominator) tells you how many equal parts the whole is cut into, and the top number (numerator) tells you how many of those parts you are taking.
